Lunar lander providers signal capacity to support NASA's accelerated moon mission cadence, presenting supply chain opportunities for aerospace contractors.

Military Mar 23, 2026

SNC’s AEGIR-W Sea Drone Washes Ashore on Turkey’s Black Sea Coast

The AEGIR-W Unmanned Surface Vessel mysteriously ran aground on the Black Sea Coast in northeastern Turkey and was later blown up by Turkish authorities. An Unmanned Surface Vessel (USV) was found washed ashore on northeastern Turkey’s Black Sea coast in the Ordu region early in the afternoon of ...

Unmanned maritime systems face recovery risks in geopolitical waters, highlighting operational security challenges for defense contractors.

Swissto12's compact GEO satellite platform enables cost-effective optical relay capabilities, offering airlines enhanced connectivity and communication infrastructure options.

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrating advanced technologies across airline, airport, and flight operations is essential to accommodate growing air traffic while meeting environmental sustainability demands.

EASA's CS-29 Amendment 12 update provides rotorcraft manufacturers with clarified certification standards in multiple formats, streamlining compliance and design processes.

Military Mar 22, 2026

The Tomcat’s Six Pack: The F-14 Six on Six Phoenix Test

Designed for fleet air defense, the F-14 was armed with a potent long-range missile and, with a powerful new radar, could engage multiple airborne targets at once. The United States Navy’s F-14 Tomcat was the most potent fleet defense aircraft of its time. Its AIM-54 Phoenix missile had a long ra...

The F-14's six-simultaneous Phoenix missile engagement capability revolutionized naval air combat doctrine through advanced multi-target radar and long-range weaponry.

Military Mar 22, 2026

U.S. Army Receives DARPA’s Optionally Piloted H-60Mx Black Hawk

The U.S. Army will put the H-60Mx and Sikorsky’s MATRIX flight autonomy software through a rigorous operational testing campaign with real world logistics in contested scenarios. The U.S. Army received on Mar. 19, 2026, the H-60Mx Black Hawk Optionally Piloted Vehicle (OPV) at Fort Eustis, Virgin...

DARPA's optionally piloted H-60Mx demonstrates autonomous helicopter viability for military logistics, signaling potential crew-reduction opportunities across rotorcraft operations.

Airlines can now bypass traditional IFC vendors through marketplace platforms, gaining direct control over connectivity selection as LEO satellite competition accelerates innovation cycles.
